---
title: "Part 2"
output: html_notebook
---


Model Fitting

Setup and Data Prep for Modelling
```{r}
#data <- readr::read_csv("heart_failure.csv")

data_model <- data |>#re-prepare data
  mutate(
    comorbidity_count = anaemia + diabetes + high_blood_pressure + smoking,
    fatal_mi = factor(fatal_mi, levels = c(1, 0), labels = c("Fatal", "Survived"))
  ) |>
  select(-time)

set.seed(100)
```


Train / Test Split Data
```{r}
n <- nrow(data_model)
train_idx <- sample(n, size = floor(0.8 * n)) #80% for evaluating models 

train_data <- data_model[train_idx, ]
test_data  <- data_model[-train_idx, ]

cat("Training set size:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
cat("Test set size:    ", nrow(test_data), "\n")
cat("Fatal event rate (train):", round(mean(train_data$fatal_mi == "Fatal") * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("Fatal event rate (test): ", round(mean(test_data$fatal_mi  == "Fatal") * 100, 1), "%\n")
```
Task Definition
```{r}
task_hf <- TaskClassif$new(id = "HeartFailure", backend = train_data, target = "fatal_mi", positive = "Fatal") # Fatal = positive class
task_hf
```

Cross-Validation Strategy
```{r}
cv5 <- rsmp("cv", folds = 5)
cv5$instantiate(task_hf)
```


Performance Measures
```{r}
as.data.table(mlr_measures) #choose measures
```

```{r}
measures <- list(
  msr("classif.ce"),         
  msr("classif.acc"),        
  msr("classif.auc"),         
  msr("classif.sensitivity"), 
  msr("classif.fnr"),         
  msr("classif.fpr")          
)
```

Check what models are available
```{r}
as.data.table(mlr_learners)
```

Models 
```{r}
lrn_baseline <- lrn("classif.featureless", predict_type = "prob")

lrn_log_reg <- lrn("classif.log_reg", predict_type = "prob")
pl_log_reg<- po("encode") %>>% po(lrn_log_reg)

lrn_ranger<- lrn("classif.ranger",  predict_type = "prob",
                     num.trees = 500, importance = "impurity")

```



All Models Compared on the Same Folds
```{r}
res <- benchmark(data.table(
  task       = list(task_hf),
  learner    = list(lrn_baseline, pl_log_reg, lrn_ranger),
  resampling = list(cv5)
), store_models = TRUE)

res$aggregate(measures)
```


Inspecting Individual Models
